title: Parent to Parent Networking * Perinatal Depression - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parent to Parent Networking * Perinatal Depression 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Fathers Foster Parents Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support for parents and birthing people coping with perinatal mood and anxiety disorders through connection, information and resources. Services include 24-hour phone support, parent to parent support groups online and in-person and partner support for family members who want to support someone coping with postpartum depression or anxiety. Resource packets may be emailed or mailed at no cost. PO Box 33128 Portland, OR 97292 Eligibility: Parents experiencing any kind of perinatal mood disorders and stress related to welcoming a new child, and their family members. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 557-8375 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES
